cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So the task is to figure out which of the given options is wrong based on the news article provided. Let me start by reading through the article carefully and then analyzing each option one by one. First, the article states that the space shuttle Columbia broke up during re-entry, killing all seven astronauts. President Bush called it a tragedy but said, "America's space program will go on." So option A says that they will give up the space program because of the accident. But according to the article, Bush actually said the opposite. So option A might be the wrong one. Let me hold that thought and check the other options. Looking at option B: NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster. The article mentions that NASA launched an investigation and that it was too early to nail down a precise cause. So B is correct as per the article, meaning it's not the wrong answer. Option C: Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing. The article says that in 42 years of US human space flight, there had never been an accident during descent or landing before Columbia. The Challenger exploded during take-off.
